CoInfoSim — Occupancy Real → Real Monte Carlo Report

Channels: X₁ = TemperatureX₂ = HumidityX₃ = LightX₄ = CO₂X₅ = Humidity Ratio
Metric. Empirical test loss (misclassification rate on the fixed real evaluation split). The test-loss scale is preserved as-is. Channel subsets use compact variable notation (see sticky legend above); full sensor names appear in metadata and appendices.

1. Arm summary

Arm: Real → Real

Training samples are drawn as balanced batches from the standardized datatraining.txt pool. Evaluation uses the fixed real Occupancy evaluation split (standardized datatest.txt + datatest2.txt).

2. Scientific role of this arm

This arm is the empirical baseline. It directly measures cooperative channel structure under real Occupancy training data. Results from the other arms are compared against this reference.

3. Dataset provenance summary

Dataset: UCI Occupancy Detection (ID 357, DOI 10.24432/C5X01N, Luis Candanedo, CC BY 4.0).
Training pool: datatraining.txt. Fixed real evaluation split: datatest.txt + datatest2.txt. Standardization parameters are estimated from the training pool only.

4. Run configuration

Arm
Real → Real
Dataset
UCI Occupancy Detection
Target
binary Occupancy label
Mode
smoke
Sample sizes (n_per_class)
[2, 4, 8, 16, 32]
Classifiers
Linear SVM, Logistic Regression, Gaussian Naive Bayes
Channel subsets evaluated
31 subsets
Fixed test set
fixed real Occupancy evaluation split (standardized datatest.txt + datatest2.txt) (12417 rows)
Min / max replications
10 / 40
Replication batch size
5
Target CI95 half-width
0.05
Base seed
0
Runtime
40.89 s

Classifier configuration

Detailed classifier configuration was not recorded for this historical result.

List all evaluated channel subsets

{X₁}, {X₂}, {X₃}, {X₄}, {X₅}, {X₁, X₂}, {X₁, X₃}, {X₁, X₄}, {X₁, X₅}, {X₂, X₃}, {X₂, X₄}, {X₂, X₅}, {X₃, X₄}, {X₃, X₅}, {X₄, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃}, {X₁, X₂, X₄}, {X₁, X₂, X₅}, {X₁, X₃, X₄}, {X₁, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₄, X₅}, {X₂, X₃, X₄}, {X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₂, X₄, X₅}, {X₃, X₄, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₄, X₅}, {X₁, X₃, X₄, X₅}, {X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅}

5. Training/evaluation protocol

Training source
Balanced draws from the standardized datatraining.txt pool
Evaluation source
Fixed real evaluation split: standardized datatest.txt + datatest2.txt
Sampling mechanism
Deterministic balanced sampling by base_seed, class label and replication ID

6. Arm-specific data and model description

No additional arm-specific content is configured for this section.

7. Reproducibility controls

Training pool
datatraining.txt
Test split
datatest.txt + datatest2.txt (concatenated)
Standardization
z-score, fitted on training pool only (ddof=0)
Sampler type
RealDatasetSampler (balanced, deterministic by seed)

8. Monte Carlo stopping rule

Standard-error stopping: replications continue in batches of 5 until the maximum 95% CI half-width (1.96 × SE) across all (subset, classifier) cells falls at or below the target (0.05) or the replication budget (40) is exhausted.

n_per_classreplicationsstopping reasonmax CI half-width
240max_budget0.0746
440max_budget0.0569
840max_budget0.0612
1640max_budget0.0513
3220converged0.0400

9. Monte Carlo precision diagnostics

Per n_per_class: the maximum standard error and the maximum 95% CI half-width (1.96 × SE) across all (subset, classifier) cells, compared against the convergence target (0.05, on the same CI95 half-width scale). All quantities preserve the empirical test-loss scale.

n_per_classReplicationsMax SEMax CI95 half-widthTarget CI95 half-widthStopping reason
2400.038040.074560.05000max_budget
4400.029020.056870.05000max_budget
8400.031230.061220.05000max_budget
16400.026150.051260.05000max_budget
32200.020420.040030.05000converged

10. Loss curves

Loss curves are available in two complementary views. Best by cardinality shows the best subset for every available dimension from 1 through 5, including the Full-5 reference. Nested cardinality shows all subsets of one selected cardinality against the same 5-channel reference. Linear SVM is shown by default; error bars show ±1 SE.

11. Best subset comparison

Interpretable comparison of the best subset at every available dimension from 1 through 5, including the full 5-channel subset. The full-channel reference row is highlighted. Select a classifier below (Linear SVM default).

13. Pairwise winner and reversal dynamics

This arm-local view shows the effective winner matrix W and the progressive reversal matrix R together over sample size. One best subset per cardinality is selected using this arm at the largest N and frozen across all displayed panels. This display reduction does not change the all-subset structural data persisted for the run.

Arm-local display selection at Nmax: {X₃}, {X₁, X₃}, {X₁, X₂, X₃}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅}. Numerical structures use all subsets.

The Winner matrix W shows who currently wins each unordered subset pair; the Reversal matrix R shows when that pair last changed winner through the selected prefix. An exact tie after a pair's first strict winner carries the previous winner forward in W rather than resetting it to unresolved. An R cell exists only for a pair with at least one valid winner reversal: a defined effective winner at both the previous and the current sample size that differs between the two.

N is the first evaluated sample size, so no reversal can yet exist: the Reversal matrix is empty.

Arm-local display selection at Nmax: {X₃}, {X₂, X₃}, {X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅}. Numerical structures use all subsets.

The Winner matrix W shows who currently wins each unordered subset pair; the Reversal matrix R shows when that pair last changed winner through the selected prefix. An exact tie after a pair's first strict winner carries the previous winner forward in W rather than resetting it to unresolved. An R cell exists only for a pair with at least one valid winner reversal: a defined effective winner at both the previous and the current sample size that differs between the two.

N is the first evaluated sample size, so no reversal can yet exist: the Reversal matrix is empty.

Arm-local display selection at Nmax: {X₃}, {X₂, X₃}, {X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₅}, {X₁, X₂, X₃, X₄, X₅}. Numerical structures use all subsets.

The Winner matrix W shows who currently wins each unordered subset pair; the Reversal matrix R shows when that pair last changed winner through the selected prefix. An exact tie after a pair's first strict winner carries the previous winner forward in W rather than resetting it to unresolved. An R cell exists only for a pair with at least one valid winner reversal: a defined effective winner at both the previous and the current sample size that differs between the two.

N is the first evaluated sample size, so no reversal can yet exist: the Reversal matrix is empty.
